definitions: Actions undertaken by a central bank to influence the availability and cost of money and credit to help promote national economic goals.
The process by which the monetary authority of a country controls the supply of money, often targeting an inflation rate or interest rate to ensure price stability and general trust in the currency.
examples: The Federal Reserve sets monetary policy in the United States.
Changes in monetary policy can affect interest rates and economic growth.
The central bank announced a shift in its monetary policy.
Tightening monetary policy aims to curb inflation.
